title: narcissus × gracilis var. princeps
intro: Narcissus × gracilis var. princeps, also known as the Prince's narcissus, is a bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its bulb, which contains alkaloids, including galantamine, responsible for its pharmacological effects. The Prince's narcissus has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including rheumatism, bronchitis, and asthma, as well as to improve cognitive function and memory. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
